title: viburnum opulus var. trilobum
intro: Viburnum opulus var. trilobum, also known as the highbush cranberry or guelder rose, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe, Western Asia, and North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Viburnum opulus var. trilobum include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce anxiety and stress, and provide antioxidant properties that help protect against cell damage. Viburnum opulus var. trilobum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in herbal remedies for its medicinal and health benefits.
